---
package: "openpipeline"
version: "v4.2.0"
title: "leiden"
namespace: "Cluster"
description: "Cluster cells using the [Leiden algorithm] [Traag18] implemented in the [Scanpy framework] [Wolf18]."
type: "module"
toc: false
latest: "true"
subtitle: "openpipeline v4.2.0"
---

::: {.column-margin}

### Info

ID: `leiden`  
Namespace: `cluster`

### Links

[{{< fa brands github >}} View source code](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline/tree/v4.2.0/src/cluster/leiden)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Browse all versions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Get run instructions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/cluster/leiden)

:::


Leiden is an improved version of the [Louvain algorithm] [Blondel08]. 
It has been proposed for single-cell analysis by [Levine15] [Levine15]. 
This requires having ran `neighbors/find_neighbors` or `neighbors/bbknn` first.

[Blondel08]: Blondel et al. (2008), Fast unfolding of communities in large networks, J. Stat. Mech.  
[Levine15]: Levine et al. (2015), Data-Driven Phenotypic Dissection of AML Reveals Progenitor-like Cells that Correlate with Prognosis, Cell.  
[Traag18]: Traag et al. (2018), From Louvain to Leiden: guaranteeing well-connected communities arXiv.  
[Wolf18]: Wolf et al. (2018), Scanpy: large-scale single-cell gene expression data analysis, Genome Biology.  


## Example commands

You can run the pipeline using `nextflow run`.

### View help

You can use `--help` as a parameter to get an overview of the possible parameters.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -main-script target/nextflow/cluster/leiden/main.nf \
  --help
```

### Run command

<details>
<summary>Example of `params.yaml`</summary>

Generate a `params.yaml` for this component on [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/cluster/leiden).

</details>

Run locally with:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/cluster/leiden/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

:::{.callout-note}
Replace `-profile docker` with `-profile podman` or `-profile singularity` depending on the desired backend.
:::

Running on Seqera or through Viash Hub is explained in the [guides](/guides/index.qmd).

## Argument group

### Arguments

|Name                    |Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |Attributes                                                    |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--input`               |Input file.                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |`file`, required, example: `"input.h5mu"`                     |
|`--modality`            |Which modality from the input MuData file to process.                                                                                                                                                                                    |`string`, default: `"rna"`                                    |
|`--obsp_connectivities` |In which .obsp slot the neighbor connectivities can be found.                                                                                                                                                                            |`string`, default: `"connectivities"`                         |
|`--output`              |Output file.                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |`file`, required, example: `"output.h5mu"`                    |
|`--obsm_name`           |Name of the .obsm key under which to add the cluster labels. The name of the columns in the matrix will correspond to the resolutions.                                                                                                   |`string`, default: `"leiden"`                                 |
|`--flavor`              |Which package's implementation to use.                                                                                                                                                                                                   |`string`, default: `"leidenalg"`                              |
|`--n_iterations`        |How many iterations of the Leiden clustering algorithm to perform. When defined, positive values above 2 define the total number of iterations to perform. When not set, the algorithm will run until it reaches its optimal clustering. |`integer`                                                     |
|`--seed`                |Fix the initialization of the optimization. Can be used to increase reproducibility.                                                                                                                                                     |`integer`                                                     |
|`--resolution`          |A parameter value controlling the coarseness of the clustering. Higher values lead to more clusters. Multiple values will result in clustering being performed multiple times.                                                           |List of `double`, required, default: `1`, multiple_sep: `";"` |
|`--output_compression`  |Compression format to use for the output AnnData and/or Mudata H5 files. By default no compression is applied.                                                                                                                           |`string`, example: `"gzip"`                                   |

## Authors


  * Dries De Maeyer [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/ddemaeyer) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/dries-de-maeyer-b46a814) (maintainer)
